Q5 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are true?
S1 : In a lattice every two elements need not have both join and meet.
S2 : In a distributive lattice L, a given element can have atmost one complement.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
S1 is false because poset would be called as lattice iff any two elements must have both meet and join.
S2 is true because if any element has complement then it must be unique.

Q9 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider set S = {10, 20, 30, 41, 52, 59, 60}. Which of the following is/are the correct partition of the given set?

Correct answer: A, C, D
Solution

(a, c, d)
A partition of a set S is a collection of disjoint non-empty subsets of S that have S as their union. For partition in (b) {10} and {10, 20, 30, 41} are not disjoint and hence is not correct partition.

Q19 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider statements:
S1 : A finite lattice is always bounded.
S2 : Complemented lattice is a proper subset of bounded lattice.
S3 : A bounded and complemented lattice may or may not be a distributive lattice.
Which of the following is/are true?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• A lattice is bounded iff the lattice has a greatest and a least element.
∴ A finite lattice is always bounded.
• Complemented lattice is defined only for bounded lattice. A bounded lattice is complemented iff atleast one complement of every element exist in lattice. An element should one or more complements.
• A complemented lattice is distributive iff every element has a unique complement.
